questions.prompt: The default position value for elements is:
questions.explanation: Everything is `position: static` until you say otherwise.
Absolute elements climb up until they find a non-static ancestor.

questions.code:
.p { position: relative; }
.c { position: absolute; top: 0; right: 0; }
questions.explanation: .p is a positioning context — .c anchors to it.
Fixed = viewport. A transformed/perspective ancestor can capture it though.

options: Relative until a scroll threshold, then fixed within its container
questions.explanation: Sticky flips to fixed once the scroll position passes the threshold you set (e.g. top: 0).

options: Shifts the element 20px DOWN from its normal spot
questions.explanation: For relative, offsets shift the element but its space is preserved.

options: Still occupies its space in normal flow
Is removed from flow — surrounding content collapses
Always creates a new stacking context
questions.explanation: Absolute/fixed are out of flow; other content ignores their space.
